8. go + kegg analysis

egg-NOG-mapper

2.1.12

# gffread -g Locusta_migratoria_v2.genome.fa -y locusta_proteins.fa Locusta_migratoria_v2_fixed.gff3
# 
# gffread -g Harmonia_axyridis.genome.fa -y harmonia_proteins.fa Harmonia_axyridis.gff3
# 
# gffread -g Plutella_xylostella.genome.fa -y plutella_proteins.fa Plutella_xylostella.gff3
# 
# gffread -g Drosophila_melanogaster.genome.fa -y drosophila_proteins.fa Drosophila_melanogaster.gff3
# 
# gffread -g Blattella_germanica.genome.fa -y blattella_proteins.fa Blattella_germanica.gff3

libraries

library(clusterProfiler)

  MRCA.phylo          tidytree

  groupOTU.treedata   tidytree

  inner_join.treedata tidytree
  is.rooted.treedata  tidytree
  nodeid.phylo        tidytree
  nodeid.treedata     tidytree
  nodelab.phylo       tidytree
  nodelab.treedata    tidytree
  offspring.phylo     tidytree
  offspring.treedata  tidytree
  parent.phylo        tidytree
  parent.treedata     tidytree

# If you use GOSemSim in published research, please cite:
# - Guangchuang Yu. Gene Ontology Semantic Similarity Analysis Using GOSemSim. In: Kidder B. (eds) Stem Cell Transcriptional Networks. Methods in Molecular Biology, 2020, 2117:207-215. Humana, New York, NY. doi:10.1007/978-1-0716-0301-7_11

set-up

dds <- readRDS("/home/cluster_user/link_to_datadisk/copy/counts/dds.rds")
gene2og <- readRDS("/home/cluster_user/link_to_datadisk/copy/counts/gene2og.rds")

background_ogs <- rownames(dds)
length(background_ogs)
[1] 821
head(background_ogs)
[1] "OG0006107" "OG0006108" "OG0006118" "OG0006123" "OG0006128" "OG0006129"
length(rownames(gene2og))/5
[1] 821
#without renaming to anything there are errors
gene2og <- gene2og %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    gene_id = "Gene",
    og = "orthogroup"
  )
stages <- c("Eggs", "Embryo", "Larva", "Nymph", "Prepupa", "Pupa", "Adult Male", "Adult Female")

all_sig_ogs <- list()

for(i in 1:(length(stages)-1)){
  for(j in (i+1):length(stages)){
    stage1 <- stages[i]
    stage2 <- stages[j]
    
    res <- results(dds, contrast = c("Stage2", stage2, stage1)) 
    
    sig_ogs <- rownames(res[which(res$padj < 0.05 & abs(res$log2FoldChange) > 1), ])
    
    contrast_name <- paste(stage2, "_vs_", stage1, sep="")
    all_sig_ogs[[contrast_name]] <- sig_ogs
  }
}

names(all_sig_ogs)
 [1] "Embryo_vs_Eggs"             "Larva_vs_Eggs"             
 [3] "Nymph_vs_Eggs"              "Prepupa_vs_Eggs"           
 [5] "Pupa_vs_Eggs"               "Adult Male_vs_Eggs"        
 [7] "Adult Female_vs_Eggs"       "Larva_vs_Embryo"           
 [9] "Nymph_vs_Embryo"            "Prepupa_vs_Embryo"         
[11] "Pupa_vs_Embryo"             "Adult Male_vs_Embryo"      
[13] "Adult Female_vs_Embryo"     "Nymph_vs_Larva"            
[15] "Prepupa_vs_Larva"           "Pupa_vs_Larva"             
[17] "Adult Male_vs_Larva"        "Adult Female_vs_Larva"     
[19] "Prepupa_vs_Nymph"           "Pupa_vs_Nymph"             
[21] "Adult Male_vs_Nymph"        "Adult Female_vs_Nymph"     
[23] "Pupa_vs_Prepupa"            "Adult Male_vs_Prepupa"     
[25] "Adult Female_vs_Prepupa"    "Adult Male_vs_Pupa"        
[27] "Adult Female_vs_Pupa"       "Adult Female_vs_Adult Male"
#lapply(all_sig_ogs, length)

GO & KEGG

Drosophila

eggnog_drosophila <- read.table("/home/cluster_user/Magisterka/drosophila.emapper.annotations.tsv",
                                sep = "\t",
                                header = FALSE,
                                comment.char = "#",
                                quote = "",
                                stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

colnames(eggnog_drosophila) <- c("query", "seed_ortholog", "evalue", "score", "eggNOG_OGs", 
                                 "max_annot_lvl", "COG_category", "Description", "Preferred_name",
                                 "GOs", "EC", "KEGG_ko", "KEGG_Pathway", "KEGG_Module",
                                 "KEGG_Reaction", "KEGG_rclass", "BRITE", "KEGG_TC",
                                 "CAZy", "BiGG_Reaction", "PFAMs")

#delete .1 from the end of names
eggnog_drosophila$query <- sub("\\.\\d+$", "", eggnog_drosophila$query)

head(eggnog_drosophila)
       query    seed_ortholog    evalue score
1 Dmen000001 7227.FBpp0297938  0.00e+00  1010

4 Dmen000003 7227.FBpp0099481  0.00e+00  1466
5 Dmen000005 7227.FBpp0070103 4.32e-259   711
6 Dmen000006 7227.FBpp0305207  0.00e+00  3175

1         RC00064,RC00090,RC00096 ko00000,ko00001,ko01000,ko01002,ko04090
2                               - ko00000,ko00001,ko01000,ko01001,ko04131

  KEGG_TC CAZy BiGG_Reaction                             PFAMs
1       -    -             -                   G_glu_transpept
2       -    -             - Bcr-Abl_Oligo,C2,PH,RhoGAP,RhoGEF
3       -    -             -                     Acyl_transf_3
4       -    -             -        PAN_1,PAN_4,Zona_pellucida
5       -    -             -                      Galactosyl_T
6       -    -             -           Ank_2,Ank_4,Pre-SET,SET
go_drosophila <- eggnog_drosophila %>%
  select(term = GOs, gene = query) %>%
  filter(!is.na(term) & term != "-") %>%
  separate_rows(term, sep = ",") %>%
  left_join(gene2og, by = c("gene" = "gene_id")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(og)) %>%
  select(term, gene = og) %>%
  distinct()

head(go_drosophila)
# A tibble: 6 × 2
  term       gene     
  <chr>      <chr>    
1 GO:0002118 OG0006538
2 GO:0002121 OG0006538
3 GO:0007275 OG0006538
4 GO:0007399 OG0006538
5 GO:0007417 OG0006538
6 GO:0007420 OG0006538
kegg_drosophila <- eggnog_drosophila %>%
  select(term = KEGG_Pathway, gene = query) %>%
  filter(!is.na(term) & term != "-") %>%
  separate_rows(term, sep = ",") %>%
  left_join(gene2og, by = c("gene" = "gene_id")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(og)) %>%
  select(term, gene = og) %>%
  distinct()

head(kegg_drosophila)
# A tibble: 6 × 2
  term     gene     
  <chr>    <chr>    
1 ko00072  OG0006538
2 ko00650  OG0006538
3 ko01100  OG0006538
4 map00072 OG0006538
5 map00650 OG0006538
6 map01100 OG0006538

Harmonia

eggnog_harmonia <- read.table("/home/cluster_user/Magisterka/harmonia.emapper.annotations.tsv",
                                sep = "\t",
                                header = FALSE,
                                comment.char = "#",
                                quote = "",
                                stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

colnames(eggnog_harmonia) <- c("query", "seed_ortholog", "evalue", "score", "eggNOG_OGs", 
                                 "max_annot_lvl", "COG_category", "Description", "Preferred_name",
                                 "GOs", "EC", "KEGG_ko", "KEGG_Pathway", "KEGG_Module",
                                 "KEGG_Reaction", "KEGG_rclass", "BRITE", "KEGG_TC",
                                 "CAZy", "BiGG_Reaction", "PFAMs")

#delete .1 from the end of names
eggnog_harmonia$query <- sub("\\.\\d+$", "", eggnog_harmonia$query)

head(eggnog_harmonia)
       query         seed_ortholog    evalue score
1 Haxy000005      7070.TC015477-PA  1.10e-92 306.0
2 Haxy000006   34740.HMEL015122-PA  3.20e-48 163.0
3 Haxy000007       136037.KDR14467  5.84e-76 242.0
4 Haxy000008 500637.PROVRUST_08181  1.60e-12  68.2
5 Haxy000009      7070.TC001793-PA 3.17e-162 520.0
6 Haxy000015      7070.TC030784-PA 5.26e-215 609.0
                                                                                                                                                                 eggNOG_OGs
1                        COG5078@1|root,KOG0895@2759|Eukaryota,38KJS@33154|Opisthokonta,3BCKT@33208|Metazoa,3CRV1@33213|Bilateria,41TTV@6656|Arthropoda,3SFS0@50557|Insecta
2 COG5245@1|root,KOG3595@2759|Eukaryota,38BFS@33154|Opisthokonta,3B9HK@33208|Metazoa,3CV0I@33213|Bilateria,41UGT@6656|Arthropoda,3SJTB@50557|Insecta,44851@7088|Lepidoptera
3                        KOG3551@1|root,KOG3551@2759|Eukaryota,38F0K@33154|Opisthokonta,3BA4X@33208|Metazoa,3CRWS@33213|Bilateria,41UJN@6656|Arthropoda,3SH1Y@50557|Insecta
4                                                          COG0689@1|root,COG0689@2|Bacteria,1MVFZ@1224|Proteobacteria,1RNTB@1236|Gammaproteobacteria,3Z8BF@586|Providencia
5                                                                                                            COG2801@1|root,KOG0017@2759|Eukaryota,38F42@33154|Opisthokonta
6                        COG0277@1|root,KOG1232@2759|Eukaryota,38DBR@33154|Opisthokonta,3BFC0@33208|Metazoa,3CYB0@33213|Bilateria,41UEM@6656|Arthropoda,3SIVX@50557|Insecta
             max_annot_lvl COG_category
1            33208|Metazoa            O
2            33208|Metazoa            Z
3            33208|Metazoa            W
4 1236|Gammaproteobacteria            J
5       33154|Opisthokonta            G
6            33208|Metazoa            C
                                                                                                                                                                                           Description
1                                                                                                                                                   Belongs to the ubiquitin-conjugating enzyme family
2                                                                                                                                                     Dynein heavy chain and region D6 of dynein motor
3                                                                                                                                                                          Pleckstrin homology domain.
4 Phosphorolytic exoribonuclease that removes nucleotide residues following the -CCA terminus of tRNA and adds nucleotides to the ends of RNA molecules by using nucleoside diphosphates as substrates
5                                                                                                                                            retrotransposable element Tf2 155 kDa protein type 1-like
6                                                                     UDP-N-acetylmuramate dehydrogenase activity. It is involved in the biological process described with oxidation-reduction process
  Preferred_name
1          UBE2O
2         DNAH10
3          SNTA1
4            rph
5              -
6         D2HGDH

GO:0000166,GO:0003674,GO:0003824,GO:0004457,GO:0004458,GO:0005488,GO:0005575,GO:0005622,GO:0005623,GO:0005737,GO:0005739,GO:0005759,GO:0006082,GO:0006089,GO:0006091,GO:0006103,GO:0006807,GO:0008150,GO:0008152,GO:0009987,GO:0010035,GO:0010038,GO:0010042,GO:0010043,GO:0015980,GO:0016491,GO:0016614,GO:0016898,GO:0019516,GO:0019538,GO:0019752,GO:0022900,GO:0022904,GO:0031974,GO:0032025,GO:0032787,GO:0036094,GO:0042221,GO:0043167,GO:0043168,GO:0043170,GO:0043226,GO:0043227,GO:0043229,GO:0043231,GO:0043233,GO:0043436,GO:0043648,GO:0044237,GO:0044238,GO:0044260,GO:0044267,GO:0044281,GO:0044422,GO:0044424,GO:0044429,GO:0044444,GO:0044446,GO:0044464,GO:0045333,GO:0048037,GO:0050660,GO:0050662,GO:0050896,GO:0051186,GO:0051990,GO:0055114,GO:0070013,GO:0071704,GO:0071949,GO:0097159,GO:1901265,GO:1901363,GO:1901564,GO:1901615
         EC   KEGG_ko     KEGG_Pathway KEGG_Module KEGG_Reaction KEGG_rclass
1  2.3.2.24 ko:K10581 ko04120,map04120           -             -           -
2         - ko:K10408 ko05016,map05016           -             -           -
3         -         -                -           -             -           -
4  2.7.7.56 ko:K00989                -           -             -           -
5         -         -                -           -             -           -
6 1.1.99.39 ko:K18204                -           -             -           -
                            BRITE KEGG_TC CAZy BiGG_Reaction
1 ko00000,ko00001,ko01000,ko04121       -    -             -
2         ko00000,ko00001,ko04812       -    -             -
3                               -       -    -             -
4         ko00000,ko01000,ko03016       -    -             -
5                               -       -    -             -
6                 ko00000,ko01000       -    -             -
                                                        PFAMs
1                                                      UQ_con
2 AAA_5,AAA_6,AAA_7,AAA_8,AAA_9,DHC_N1,DHC_N2,Dynein_heavy,MT
3                                                      PDZ,PH
4                                         RNase_PH,RNase_PH_C
5                          Retrotrans_gag,rve,zf-CCHC,zf-H2C2
6                                 FAD-oxidase_C,FAD_binding_4
go_harmonia <- eggnog_harmonia %>%
  select(term = GOs, gene = query) %>%
  filter(!is.na(term) & term != "-") %>%
  separate_rows(term, sep = ",") %>%
  left_join(gene2og, by = c("gene" = "gene_id")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(og)) %>%
  select(term, gene = og) %>%
  distinct()

head(go_harmonia)
# A tibble: 6 × 2
  term       gene     
  <chr>      <chr>    
1 GO:0005575 OG0006219
2 GO:0005622 OG0006219
3 GO:0005623 OG0006219
4 GO:0005737 OG0006219
5 GO:0005783 OG0006219
6 GO:0005789 OG0006219
kegg_harmonia <- eggnog_harmonia %>%
  select(term = KEGG_Pathway, gene = query) %>%
  filter(!is.na(term) & term != "-") %>%
  separate_rows(term, sep = ",") %>%
  left_join(gene2og, by = c("gene" = "gene_id")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(og)) %>%
  select(term, gene = og) %>%
  distinct()

head(kegg_harmonia)
# A tibble: 6 × 2
  term     gene     
  <chr>    <chr>    
1 ko00030  OG0000250
2 ko00260  OG0000250
3 ko01100  OG0000250
4 ko01110  OG0000250
5 ko01130  OG0000250
6 map00030 OG0000250

Locusta

# head -n 4 /home/cluster_user/Magisterka/locusta1.emapper.annotations.tsv > /home/cluster_user/Magisterka/locusta.emapper.annotations.tsv  
# tail -n +5 /home/cluster_user/Magisterka/locusta1.emapper.annotations.tsv >> /home/cluster_user/Magisterka/locusta.emapper.annotations.tsv
# tail -n +5 /home/cluster_user/Magisterka/locusta2.emapper.annotations.tsv >> /home/cluster_user/Magisterka/locusta.emapper.annotations.tsv
# tail -n +5 /home/cluster_user/Magisterka/locusta3.emapper.annotations.tsv >> /home/cluster_user/Magisterka/locusta.emapper.annotations.tsv
# tail -n +5 /home/cluster_user/Magisterka/locusta4.emapper.annotations.tsv >> /home/cluster_user/Magisterka/locusta.emapper.annotations.tsv
# tail -n +5 /home/cluster_user/Magisterka/locusta5.emapper.annotations.tsv >> /home/cluster_user/Magisterka/locusta.emapper.annotations.tsv
eggnog_locusta <- read.table("/home/cluster_user/Magisterka/locusta.emapper.annotations.tsv",
                                sep = "\t",
                                header = FALSE,
                                comment.char = "#",
                                quote = "",
                                stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

colnames(eggnog_locusta) <- c("query", "seed_ortholog", "evalue", "score", "eggNOG_OGs", 
                                 "max_annot_lvl", "COG_category", "Description", "Preferred_name",
                                 "GOs", "EC", "KEGG_ko", "KEGG_Pathway", "KEGG_Module",
                                 "KEGG_Reaction", "KEGG_rclass", "BRITE", "KEGG_TC",
                                 "CAZy", "BiGG_Reaction", "PFAMs")

#delete .t1 .t2 from the end of names
eggnog_locusta$query <- sub("\\.t\\d+$", "", eggnog_locusta$query)

head(eggnog_locusta)
  query        seed_ortholog    evalue score
1   g16      2340.JV46_28950 2.25e-251 747.0

4   g57 121225.PHUM016580-PA  4.17e-13  69.3
5   g61  7739.XP_002590906.1  9.48e-31 117.0
6   g62      136037.KDQ97276 9.48e-116 367.0

go_locusta <- eggnog_locusta %>%
  select(term = GOs, gene = query) %>%
  filter(!is.na(term) & term != "-") %>%
  separate_rows(term, sep = ",") %>%
  left_join(gene2og, by = c("gene" = "gene_id")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(og)) %>%
  select(term, gene = og) %>%
  distinct()

eggnog_blattella <- read_excel("/home/cluster_user/Magisterka/blattella.emapper.annotations.xlsx", skip =2)


colnames(eggnog_blattella) <- c("query", "seed_ortholog", "evalue", "score", "eggNOG_OGs", 
                                 "max_annot_lvl", "COG_category", "Description", "Preferred_name",
                                 "GOs", "EC", "KEGG_ko", "KEGG_Pathway", "KEGG_Module",

impactful genes

dds <- readRDS("/home/cluster_user/link_to_datadisk/copy/counts/dds_batch.rds")

vst <- varianceStabilizingTransformation(dds, blind = FALSE)

functions - correlation and enrichement

run_stage_correlation <- function(expr_mat, stage_vector, method = "spearman") {
  #Calculate gene-wise correlation with developmental stage order
  
  #check that sample number matches stage vector length
  stopifnot(ncol(expr_mat) == length(stage_vector))
  
  #calculate correlation and p-value for each gene
  cor_res <- apply(expr_mat, 1, function(gene_expr) {
    ct <- suppressWarnings(
      cor.test(gene_expr, stage_vector, method = method)
    )
    c(rho = unname(ct$estimate), p = ct$p.value)
  })
  
  #convert results to dataframe
  cor_df <- as.data.frame(t(cor_res))
  cor_df$gene_id <- rownames(cor_df)
  
  #adjust p-values for multiple testing
  cor_df$padj <- p.adjust(cor_df$p, method = "BH")
  
  return(cor_df)
}

classify_trend_genes <- function(cor_df, rho_cutoff = 0.5, padj_cutoff = 0.05) {
  #classify genes showing strong early or late developmental trends
  
  cor_df %>%
    mutate(
      trend = case_when(
        padj < padj_cutoff & rho >=  rho_cutoff ~ "Late",
        padj < padj_cutoff & rho <= -rho_cutoff ~ "Early",
        TRUE ~ "Other"
      )
    )
}

run_gsea <- function(cor_df, term2gene,
                                minGSSize = 10,
                                maxGSSize = 500,
                                pvalueCutoff = 0.05) {
  #Run gene set enrichment analysis using ranked gene–stage correlations
  
  #create ranked gene list using correlation coefficient
  gene_list <- cor_df %>%
    arrange(desc(rho)) %>%
    select(gene_id, rho)
  
  #convert to named numeric vector required by GSEA
  gene_list_vec <- setNames(gene_list$rho, gene_list$gene_id)
  gene_list_vec <- sort(gene_list_vec, decreasing = TRUE)
  
  #run GSEA
  gsea_res <- GSEA(
    geneList = gene_list_vec,
    TERM2GENE = term2gene,
    minGSSize = minGSSize,
    maxGSSize = maxGSSize,
    pvalueCutoff = pvalueCutoff,
    verbose = TRUE
  )
  
  #convert result to dataframe and classify enrichment direction
  as.data.frame(gsea_res) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(direction = ifelse(NES > 0, "Late-associated", "Early-associated"))
}

annotate_go_terms <- function(gsea_df) {
  #Add GO term names and ontology categories to GSEA results
  
  #helper function returning GO term name
  get_term <- function(go_id) {
    term <- GO.db::GOTERM[[go_id]]
    if (is.null(term)) NA_character_ else Term(term)
  }
  
  #helper function returning GO ontology (BP/MF/CC)
  get_ontology <- function(go_id) {
    term <- GO.db::GOTERM[[go_id]]
    if (is.null(term)) NA_character_ else Ontology(term)
  }
  
  #annotate GO IDs with term name and ontology
  gsea_df %>%
    mutate(
      Term = vapply(ID, get_term, character(1)),
      Ontology = vapply(ID, get_ontology, character(1))
    )
}

nymph

# Expression matrix
expr <- assay(vst)

# Metadata
sample_info <- as.data.frame(colData(vst))
sample_info$Sample <- rownames(sample_info)

sample_info <- sample_info %>%
  mutate(
    instar_number = case_when(
      Stage2 == "Nymph" & grepl("\\d+", Stage) ~
        as.numeric(gsub(".*?(\\d+).*", "\\1", Stage)),
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )
Warning: There was 1 warning in `mutate()`.
ℹ In argument: `instar_number = case_when(...)`.
Caused by warning:
! NAs introduced by coercion
#Nymph samples with species-specific ordering + normalization
nymph_samples <- sample_info %>%
  filter(Stage2 == "Nymph", !is.na(instar_number)) %>%
  group_by(Species) %>%
  mutate(
    instar_rank   = dense_rank(instar_number),
    instar_scaled = instar_rank / max(instar_rank)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

expr_nymph <- expr[, nymph_samples$Sample]
instar     <- nymph_samples$instar_scaled


#Correlation
gene_cor <- run_stage_correlation(expr_nymph, instar)
gene_cor <- classify_trend_genes(gene_cor)


#correlation distribution
ggplot(gene_cor, aes(rho)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.05, fill = "pink", color = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    x = "Spearman correlation with relative nymphal development",
    y = "Number of genes",
    title = "Monotonic gene expression trends across nymphal development"
  )
Warning: Removed 2 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_bin()`).

#long format data
expr_long <- expr_nymph %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("gene_id") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    -gene_id,
    names_to = "Sample",
    values_to = "expression"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    gene_cor %>% select(gene_id, rho),
    by = "gene_id"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    nymph_samples %>% select(Sample, instar_scaled),
    by = "Sample"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    trend = case_when(
      rho >  0.5 ~ "Late",
      rho < -0.5 ~ "Early",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  )# %>%
  #filter(trend != "Other")

#trend plot
ggplot(expr_long, aes(instar_scaled, expression, color = trend, fill = trend)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2) +
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = function(x) {
      data.frame(
        y     = median(x),
        ymin = quantile(x, 0.25),
        ymax = quantile(x, 0.75)
      )
    },
    geom  = "ribbon",
    alpha = 0.1,
    color = NA
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    x = "Normalized nymphal development (0-1 scale)",
    y = "VST expression",
    title = "Opposite expression trajectories of early and late genes",
    color = "Gene group",
    fill  = "Gene group"
  )

gsea

#GSEA
gsea_go_df <- run_gsea(gene_cor, t2g_go_common)
preparing geneSet collections...
GSEA analysis...
Warning in preparePathwaysAndStats(pathways, stats, minSize, maxSize, gseaParam, : There are ties in the preranked stats (8.79% of the list).
The order of those tied genes will be arbitrary, which may produce unexpected results.
Warning in fgseaMultilevel(pathways = pathways, stats = stats, minSize =
minSize, : For some of the pathways the P-values were likely overestimated. For
such pathways log2err is set to NA.
Warning in fgseaMultilevel(pathways = pathways, stats = stats, minSize =
minSize, : For some pathways, in reality P-values are less than 1e-10. You can
set the `eps` argument to zero for better estimation.
leading edge analysis...
done...
gsea_go_annotated <- annotate_go_terms(gsea_go_df)

gsea_plot_df <- gsea_go_annotated %>%
  filter(
    p.adjust < 0.05,
    !is.na(Term),
    !is.na(NES)
  ) %>%
  group_by(direction) %>%
  slice_max(abs(NES), n = 20) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    #Term = stringr::str_wrap(Term, width = 40),
    direction = factor(direction,
      levels = c("Early-associated", "Late-associated")
    )
  )

p <- ggplot(
  gsea_plot_df,
  aes(
    x = NES,
    y = reorder_within(Term, NES, direction),
    size = -log10(p.adjust)
  )
) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey70") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8, color = "black") +
  tidytext::scale_y_reordered() +
  facet_grid(direction ~ ., scales = "free", space = "free_x") +
  scale_size(range = c(2,6)) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  labs(
    x = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
    y = "GO term",
    size = "-log10(FDR)",
    title = "Functional context of genes driving nymphal developmental trends"
  ) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.2, "cm")
  )

print(p)

ggsave(
  "/home/cluster_user/Magisterka/plots/nymph_gsea.png",
  p,
  width = 10,
  height = 10,
  dpi = 300
)

gsea_plot_df_bp <- gsea_go_annotated %>%
  filter(
    Ontology == "BP",
    p.adjust < 0.05,
    !is.na(Term),
    !is.na(NES)
  ) %>%
  group_by(direction) %>%
  slice_max(abs(NES), n = 20) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    Term = stringr::str_wrap(Term, width = 40),
    direction = factor(direction,
      levels = c("Early-associated", "Late-associated")
    )
  )

p_bp <- ggplot(
  gsea_plot_df_bp,
  aes(
    x = NES,
    y = reorder_within(Term, NES, direction),
    size = -log10(p.adjust)
  )
) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey70") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8, color = "black") +
  facet_wrap(~ direction, scales = "free_x") +
  scale_y_reordered() +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  labs(
    x = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
    y = "GO biological process",
    size = "-log10(FDR)",
    title = "Biological processes associated with gene expression trends\nacross nymphal development"
  )

print(p_bp)

ggsave(
  "/home/cluster_user/Magisterka/plots/nymph_gsea_bp.png",
  p_bp,
  width = 10,
  height = 10,
  dpi = 300
)
# Extract core enrichment genes per GO
gsea_go_genes <- gsea_go_annotated %>%
  select(ID, core_enrichment) %>%
  mutate(
    gene_list = strsplit(core_enrichment, "/")
  )

# Compute pairwise Jaccard similarity
jaccard_matrix <- outer(
  gsea_go_genes$gene_list,
  gsea_go_genes$gene_list,
  Vectorize(function(x, y) length(intersect(x, y)) / length(union(x, y)))
)

# Hierarchical clustering
hc <- hclust(as.dist(1 - jaccard_matrix))
plot(hc)

# Cut into clusters (e.g., 0.7 similarity threshold)
clusters <- cutree(hc, h = 0.3)

gsea_go_annotated$cluster <- clusters

# Pick one representative per cluster (highest NES)
gsea_collapsed <- gsea_go_annotated %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_max(NES, n = 1) %>%
  ungroup()

holometabolic

# Expression matrix
expr <- assay(vst)

# Metadata
sample_info <- as.data.frame(colData(vst))
sample_info$Sample <- rownames(sample_info)

sample_info <- sample_info %>% mutate(
  larva_inst = case_when(
    Stage2 == "Larva" & grepl("\\d+", Stage) ~
      as.numeric(gsub(".*?(\\d+).*", "\\1", Stage)),
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  ),
  
  prepupa_rank = case_when(
    Stage2 == "Prepupa" & grepl("\\d+", Sample2) ~
      as.numeric(gsub(".*?(\\d+).*", "\\1", Sample2)),
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  ),
  
  pupa_rank = case_when(
    Stage2 == "Pupa" & grepl("\\d+", Stage) ~
      as.numeric(gsub(".*?(\\d+).*", "\\1", Stage)),
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )
)
Warning: There were 3 warnings in `mutate()`.
The first warning was:
ℹ In argument: `larva_inst = case_when(...)`.
Caused by warning:
! NAs introduced by coercion
ℹ Run `dplyr::last_dplyr_warnings()` to see the 2 remaining warnings.
sample_info <- sample_info %>%
  group_by(Species) %>%
  mutate(
    max_larva   = max(larva_inst, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_prepupa = max(prepupa_rank, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    dev_order = case_when(
      Stage2 == "Larva"   ~ larva_inst,
      Stage2 == "Prepupa" ~ prepupa_rank + max_larva,
      Stage2 == "Pupa"    ~ pupa_rank + max_larva + max_prepupa,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
Warning: There were 4 warnings in `mutate()`.
The first warning was:
ℹ In argument: `max_larva = max(larva_inst, na.rm = TRUE)`.
ℹ In group 1: `Species = blattella`.
Caused by warning in `max()`:
! no non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf
ℹ Run `dplyr::last_dplyr_warnings()` to see the 3 remaining warnings.
#normalize within species
sample_info <- sample_info %>%
  group_by(Species) %>%
  mutate(dev_scaled = dev_order / max(dev_order, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()
Warning: There were 2 warnings in `mutate()`.
The first warning was:
ℹ In argument: `dev_scaled = dev_order/max(dev_order, na.rm = TRUE)`.
ℹ In group 1: `Species = blattella`.
Caused by warning in `max()`:
! no non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf
ℹ Run `dplyr::last_dplyr_warnings()` to see the 1 remaining warning.
# Keep only holometabolic samples
holo_samples <- sample_info %>%
  filter(Stage2 %in% c("Larva", "Prepupa", "Pupa"),
         !is.na(dev_scaled))

expr_holo <- expr[, holo_samples$Sample]
dev_order <- holo_samples$dev_scaled


#Correlation
gene_cor_holo <- run_stage_correlation(expr_holo, dev_order)
gene_cor_holo <- classify_trend_genes(gene_cor_holo)


#correlation distribution
ggplot(gene_cor_holo, aes(rho)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.05, fill = "lightblue", color = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    x = "Spearman correlation with normalized holometabolous development",
    y = "Number of genes",
    title = "Monotonic gene expression trends across holometabolous development"
  )

#long format data
expr_long_holo <- expr_holo %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("gene_id") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    -gene_id,
    names_to = "Sample",
    values_to = "expression"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    gene_cor_holo %>% select(gene_id, rho),
    by = "gene_id"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    holo_samples %>% select(Sample, dev_scaled),
    by = "Sample"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    trend = case_when(
      rho >  0.5 ~ "Late",
      rho < -0.5 ~ "Early",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  )

#trend plot
ggplot(expr_long_holo, aes(dev_scaled, expression, color = trend, fill = trend)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2) +
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = function(x) {
      data.frame(
        y = median(x),
        ymin = quantile(x, 0.25),
        ymax = quantile(x, 0.75)
      )
    },
    geom = "ribbon",
    alpha = 0.1,
    color = NA
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    x = "Normalized holometabolic development (0-1 scale)",
    y = "VST expression",
    title = "Opposite expression trajectories of early and late genes across larva-prepupa-pupa",
    color = "Gene group",
    fill  = "Gene group"
  )

gsea

#GSEA
gsea_go_holo_df <- run_gsea(gene_cor_holo, t2g_go_common)
preparing geneSet collections...
GSEA analysis...
Warning in preparePathwaysAndStats(pathways, stats, minSize, maxSize, gseaParam, : There are ties in the preranked stats (0.12% of the list).
The order of those tied genes will be arbitrary, which may produce unexpected results.
leading edge analysis...
done...
# Annotate GSEA results
gsea_go_holo_annotated <- annotate_go_terms(gsea_go_holo_df)

# Full GO plot (all ontologies)
gsea_plot_holo_df <- gsea_go_holo_annotated %>%
  filter(
    p.adjust < 0.05,
    !is.na(Term),
    !is.na(NES)
  ) %>%
  group_by(direction) %>%
  slice_max(abs(NES), n = 20) %>%  # keep top 20 GO per panel
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    Term = str_wrap(Term, width = 40),
    direction = factor(direction, levels = c("Early-associated", "Late-associated"))
  )

p_holo <- ggplot(
  gsea_plot_holo_df,
  aes(
    x = NES,
    y = reorder_within(Term, NES, direction),
    size = -log10(p.adjust)
  )
) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey70") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8, color = "black") +
  tidytext::scale_y_reordered() +
  facet_grid(direction ~ ., scales = "free_x", space = "free") +
  scale_size(range = c(2,6)) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  labs(
    x = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
    y = "GO term",
    size = "-log10(FDR)",
    title = "Functional context of genes driving holometabolic developmental trends"
  ) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
    panel.spacing = unit(1, "cm")
  )

print(p_holo)

ggsave(
  "/home/cluster_user/Magisterka/plots/holo_gsea.png",
  p_holo,
  width = 12,
  height = 10,
  dpi = 300
)

# Biological Processes only
gsea_plot_holo_bp <- gsea_go_holo_annotated %>%
  filter(
    Ontology == "BP",
    p.adjust < 0.05,
    !is.na(Term),
    !is.na(NES)
  ) %>%
  group_by(direction) %>%
  slice_max(abs(NES), n = 20) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    Term = str_wrap(Term, width = 40),
    direction = factor(direction, levels = c("Early-associated", "Late-associated"))
  )

p_holo_bp <- ggplot(
  gsea_plot_holo_bp,
  aes(
    x = NES,
    y = reorder_within(Term, NES, direction),
    size = -log10(p.adjust)
  )
) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey70") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8, color = "black") +
  tidytext::scale_y_reordered() +
  facet_grid(direction ~ ., scales = "free_x", space = "free") +
  scale_size(range = c(2,6)) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  labs(
    x = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
    y = "GO biological process",
    size = "-log10(FDR)",
    title = "Biological processes associated with gene expression trends\nacross holometabolic development"
  ) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
    panel.spacing = unit(1, "cm")
  )

print(p_holo_bp)

ggsave(
  "/home/cluster_user/Magisterka/plots/holo_gsea_bp.png",
  p_holo_bp,
  width = 12,
  height = 10,
  dpi = 300
)

common GOs

sig_nymph <- gsea_go_annotated %>%
  select(ID, Term, direction, NES)

sig_holo <- gsea_go_holo_annotated %>%
  select(ID, Term, direction, NES)

common_go <- intersect(sig_nymph$ID, sig_holo$ID)
length(common_go)
[1] 10
common_df <- sig_nymph %>%
  filter(ID %in% common_go) %>%
  left_join(sig_holo %>% select(ID, direction, NES), by = "ID", suffix = c("_nymph", "_holo"))

head(common_df)
          ID                               Term  direction_nymph NES_nymph
1 GO:1902494                  catalytic complex  Late-associated  1.941862
2 GO:0022857 transmembrane transporter activity Early-associated -1.907881
3 GO:0005215               transporter activity Early-associated -1.886224
4 GO:1990234                transferase complex  Late-associated  1.967390
5 GO:0030182             neuron differentiation Early-associated -1.669668
6 GO:0055085            transmembrane transport Early-associated -1.806904

1 Early-associated -2.294483

5  Late-associated  1.624217
6 Early-associated -1.858509

DEG - separate for species

based on Stage2 (no locusta)

deseq for stage vs all other stages

run_stage_vs_all_deseq <- function(counts,
                                   metadata,
                                   stage_column = "Stage2",
                                   min_count = 10,
                                   padj_cutoff = 0.05,
                                   lfc_cutoff = 0) {
  #creating DESeqDataSet
  dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
    countData = counts,
    colData = metadata,
    design = as.formula(paste0("~ ", stage_column))
  )
  
  #low reads filtering
  keep <- rowSums(counts(dds)) >= min_count
  dds <- dds[keep, ]
  
  #run DESeq
  dds <- DESeq(dds)
  
  stages <- levels(colData(dds)[[stage_column]])
  deg_list <- list()
  
  #check contrast for each stage vs all other stages
  for (st in stages) {
    
    dds_tmp <- dds
    
    dds_tmp$Stage_vs_All <- ifelse(
      colData(dds_tmp)[[stage_column]] == st,
      st,
      "other"
    )
    
    dds_tmp$Stage_vs_All <- factor(dds_tmp$Stage_vs_All)
    design(dds_tmp) <- ~ Stage_vs_All
    
    dds_tmp <- DESeq(dds_tmp, quiet = TRUE)
    
    res <- results(dds_tmp,
                   contrast = c("Stage_vs_All", st, "other"))
    
    deg_list[[paste0(st, "_vs_all")]] <-
      subset(res,
             padj < padj_cutoff &
             log2FoldChange > lfc_cutoff)
  }
  
  return(list(
    dds = dds,
    deg_stage2 = deg_list
  ))
}

enrichement - GO

run_stage_go_enrichment <- function(deg_list, eggnog_table) {
  # build GO - gene mapping
  go_species <- eggnog_table %>%
    select(term = GOs, gene = query) %>%
    filter(!is.na(term) & term != "-") %>%
    separate_rows(term, sep = ",") %>%
    distinct()

  gene2GO <- split(go_species$term, go_species$gene)

  background_genes <- unique(go_species$gene)

  # FINAL OUTPUT: store full topGO objects
  stage_topGO_objects <- list()

  for (st in names(deg_list)) {

    deg_genes <- rownames(deg_list[[st]])
    deg_genes <- deg_genes[deg_genes %in% background_genes]

    cat(st, ":", length(deg_genes), "genes used for enrichment\n")

    # binary gene list
    geneList <- factor(as.integer(background_genes %in% deg_genes))
    names(geneList) <- background_genes

    # topGO object
    GOdata <- new("topGOdata",
                  ontology = "BP",
                  allGenes = geneList,
                  annot = annFUN.gene2GO,
                  gene2GO = gene2GO)

    # enrichment test
    result <- runTest(GOdata,
                      algorithm = "weight01",
                      statistic = "fisher")

    # store EVERYTHING needed for plotting graph
    stage_topGO_objects[[st]] <- list(
      GOdata = GOdata,
      result = result,
      geneList = geneList
    )
  }

  return(stage_topGO_objects)
}

enrichement - kegg pathway

doesnt work well

visualisation

plot_stage_go_results <- function(stage_go_results,
                                  deg_list,
                                  top_n = 30,
                                  target_level = 4,
                                  output_dir = NULL,
                                  prefix = "GO") {
  # Species labels
  species_labels <- c(
    "blattella" = "B. germanica",
    "drosophila" = "D. melanogaster",
    "harmonia" = "H. axyridis",
    "locusta" = "L. migratoria",
    "plutella" = "P. xylostella",
    "drosophila2" = "D. melanogaster (set 2)"
  )
  
  # get species name (fallback to prefix if missing)
  species_name <- species_labels[[prefix]]
  if (is.null(species_name)) species_name <- prefix

  # -----------------------------
  # HELPERS
  # -----------------------------

  # check if topGO object is valid
  is_valid_obj <- function(x) {
    is.list(x) && !is.null(x$GOdata) && !is.null(x$result)
  }

  # safely convert GO ID -> term name
  safe_term <- function(go) {
    if (is.na(go)) return(NA)
    term <- tryCatch(Term(GOTERM[[go]]), error = function(e) NA)
    ifelse(is.na(term), go, term)
  }

  # -----------------------------
  # GO LEVEL ESTIMATION
  # -----------------------------
  # approximate GO level by following ONE parent path upward
  # NOTE: GO is a DAG, so level is not unique → this is approximation

  get_go_level_fast <- function(go) {
    if (go == "GO:0008150") return(1)  # BP root

    level <- 1
    current <- go

    repeat {
      parents <- GOBPPARENTS[[current]]

      if (is.null(parents) || all(is.na(parents))) break

      # take first available parent (fast approximation)
      current <- parents[!is.na(parents)][1]
      level <- level + 1

      # safety stop
      if (level > 100) break
    }

    return(level)
  }

  # -----------------------------
  # MAP GO TO TARGET LEVEL
  # -----------------------------
  # 1. take GO + all ancestors
  # 2. estimate their levels
  # 3. choose term closest to target_level

  map_to_level_fast <- function(go, target_level = 4) {

    ancestors <- c(go, unlist(GOBPANCESTOR[[go]]))
    ancestors <- ancestors[grepl("^GO:", ancestors)]

    if (length(ancestors) == 0) return(NA)

    levels <- sapply(ancestors, get_go_level_fast)

    # remove NA levels (rare but possible)
    valid <- !is.na(levels)
    ancestors <- ancestors[valid]
    levels <- levels[valid]

    if (length(levels) == 0) return(NA)

    best <- ancestors[which.min(abs(levels - target_level))]

    return(best)
  }

  plots <- list()

  # -----------------------------
  # 1. DEG COUNTS
  # -----------------------------
  stage_gene_counts <- sapply(deg_list, function(x) {
    if (is.null(x)) return(NA)
    if (is.data.frame(x) || is.matrix(x)) return(nrow(x))
    if (!is.null(rownames(x))) return(length(rownames(x)))
    return(NA)
  })

  df_counts <- data.frame(
    Stage = gsub("_vs_all", "", names(stage_gene_counts)),
    Genes = as.numeric(stage_gene_counts)
  )

  df_counts <- df_counts[!is.na(df_counts$Genes), ]

  p_counts <- ggplot(df_counts, aes(Stage, Genes, fill = Stage)) +
    geom_col() +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = paste("Number of DEGs –", species_name),
         y = "DEGs") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

  print(p_counts)
  plots[["deg_counts"]] <- p_counts

  if (!is.null(output_dir)) {
    ggsave(file.path(output_dir, paste0(prefix, "_DEG_counts.png")),
           p_counts, width = 6, height = 4)
  }

  # -----------------------------
  # 2. EXTRACT TOP GO TERMS
  # -----------------------------
  all_terms <- lapply(names(stage_go_results), function(st) {

    obj <- stage_go_results[[st]]
    if (!is_valid_obj(obj)) return(NULL)

    gt <- GenTable(
      obj$GOdata,
      Fisher = obj$result,
      orderBy = "Fisher",
      topNodes = top_n
    )

    if (is.null(gt) || nrow(gt) == 0) return(NULL)

    data.frame(
      GO = gt$GO.ID,
      score = -log10(as.numeric(gt$Fisher)),
      Stage = gsub("_vs_all", "", st),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })

  all_terms <- dplyr::bind_rows(all_terms)

  if (is.null(all_terms) || nrow(all_terms) == 0) {
    warning("No GO terms found")
    return(plots)
  }

  # -----------------------------
  # 3. MAP TO TARGET LEVEL
  # -----------------------------
  all_terms$MappedGO <- sapply(all_terms$GO,
                              map_to_level_fast,
                              target_level = target_level)

  all_terms$MappedName <- sapply(all_terms$MappedGO, safe_term)

  # -----------------------------
  # 4. COLLAPSE TERMS
  # -----------------------------
  # Multiple GO terms can map to same parent → keep strongest signal

  heatmap_df <- all_terms %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(MappedName), !is.na(score)) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(Stage, MappedName) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(score = max(score), .groups = "drop")

  # remove invalid values (fix for gray tiles)
  heatmap_df <- heatmap_df %>%
    dplyr::filter(is.finite(score))

  # keep top_n AFTER collapsing
  heatmap_df <- heatmap_df %>%
    dplyr::group_by(Stage) %>%
    dplyr::slice_max(order_by = score, n = top_n) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

  # -----------------------------
  # 5. HEATMAP
  # -----------------------------
  p_heatmap <- ggplot(heatmap_df,
                      aes(x = Stage,
                          y = reorder(stringr::str_wrap(MappedName, 40), score),
                          fill = score)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white") +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#E66101") +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = paste("GO Biological Process –", species_name),
         x = "Stage",
         y = "",
         fill = "-log10(p)") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7))

  print(p_heatmap)
  plots[["heatmap"]] <- p_heatmap

  if (!is.null(output_dir)) {
    ggsave(file.path(output_dir,
                     paste0(prefix, "_GO_heatmap.png")),
           p_heatmap, width = 8, height = 6)
  }

  return(plots)
}

#          y = "",
#          fill = "-log10(p)") +
#     theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
#           axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7))
# 
#   print(p_heatmap)
#   plots[["heatmap"]] <- p_heatmap
# 
#   if (!is.null(output_dir)) {
#     ggsave(file.path(output_dir,
#                      paste0(prefix, "_GO_heatmap.png")),
#            p_heatmap, width = 8, height = 6)
#   }
# 
#   return(plots)
# }

run

run_stage_analysis <- function(counts,
                               metadata,
                               eggnog_table,
                               stage_column = "Stage2") {
  # 1. DESEQ ANALYSIS
  res <- run_stage_vs_all_deseq(
    counts = counts,
    metadata = metadata,
    stage_column = stage_column
  )

  # extract stage-specific DEGs
  deg_stage2 <- res$deg_stage2

  # optionally keep dds (commented out to save memory)
  # dds <- res$dds

  # print number of DEGs per stage
  cat("\nDEGs per stage:\n")
  print(sapply(deg_stage2, nrow))

  # 2. OVERLAP MATRIX
  stage_gene_lists <- lapply(deg_stage2, rownames)

  overlap_matrix <- outer(
    names(stage_gene_lists),
    names(stage_gene_lists),
    Vectorize(function(x, y)
      length(intersect(stage_gene_lists[[x]], stage_gene_lists[[y]])))
  )

  cat("\nOverlap matrix:\n")
  print(overlap_matrix)

  # 3. GO ENRICHMENT
  go_results <- run_stage_go_enrichment(
    deg_list = deg_stage2,
    eggnog_table = eggnog_table
  )

  return(list(
    deg_stage2 = deg_stage2,
    go_results = go_results
  ))
}

harmonia

# load count matrix and metadata
harmonia_counts <- readRDS("/home/cluster_user/link_to_datadisk/copy/counts/harmonia_counts.rds")
harmonia_metadata <- readRDS("/home/cluster_user/link_to_datadisk/copy/counts/harmonia_metadata.rds")

# load full metadata table
metadata <- read.csv(file="/home/cluster_user/Magisterka/metadata2.csv",
                     row.names = 1,
                     header = TRUE,
                     sep = ",")

# reorder metadata rows to match count matrix columns
harmonia_metadata <- metadata[colnames(harmonia_counts), ]

harmonia <- run_stage_analysis(
  counts = harmonia_counts,
  metadata = harmonia_metadata,
  eggnog_table = eggnog_harmonia
)

  stage_go_results = harmonia_go_results,
  deg_list = harmonia_deg_stage2,
  top_n = 15,
  target_level = 4,
  output_dir = "/home/cluster_user/Magisterka/plots/",
  prefix = "harmonia"
)

blattella

blattella_counts <- readRDS("/home/cluster_user/link_to_datadisk/copy/counts/blattella_counts.rds")
blattella_metadata <- readRDS("/home/cluster_user/link_to_datadisk/copy/counts/blattella_metadata.rds")
metadata <- read.csv(file="/home/cluster_user/Magisterka/metadata2.csv", row.names = 1, header = TRUE, sep = ",") 

blattella_metadata <- metadata[colnames(blattella_counts), ]

blattella <- run_stage_analysis(
  counts = blattella_counts,
  metadata = blattella_metadata,
  eggnog_table = eggnog_blattella
)

blattella_deg_stage2 <- blattella$deg_stage2
blattella_go_results <- blattella$go_results
plots_b <- plot_stage_go_results(
  stage_go_results = blattella_go_results,
  deg_list = blattella_deg_stage2,
  top_n = 15,
  target_level = 4,
  output_dir = "/home/cluster_user/Magisterka/plots/",
  prefix = "blattella"
)

## this hemimetabolic embryo has a lot of similar processes to larvae
## nymphs has cuticle which was in pupa

compare

# function converting gene IDs to orthogroup IDs for a given species
genes_to_og <- function(genes, gene2og, species_name) {
  gene2og %>%
    filter(Species == species_name,
           gene_id %in% genes) %>%
    pull(og) %>%
    unique()
}


# convert stage-specific genes to orthogroups (Harmonia)
harmonia_stage_og <- lapply(
  harmonia_deg_stage2,
  function(df) genes_to_og(rownames(df), gene2og, "harmonia")
)

# rename stages and remove "_vs_all"
names(harmonia_stage_og) <- paste0("Harmonia_", gsub("_vs_all", "", names(harmonia_stage_og)))


# convert stage-specific genes to orthogroups (Plutella)
plutella_stage_og <- lapply(
  plutella_deg_stage2,
  function(df) genes_to_og(rownames(df), gene2og, "plutella")
)

names(plutella_stage_og) <- paste0("Plutella_", gsub("_vs_all", "", names(plutella_stage_og)))


# convert stage-specific genes to orthogroups (Drosophila dataset 1)
drosophila_stage_og <- lapply(
  drosophila_deg_stage2,
  function(df) genes_to_og(rownames(df), gene2og, "drosophila")
)

names(drosophila_stage_og) <- paste0("Drosophila_", gsub("_vs_all", "", names(drosophila_stage_og)))


# convert stage-specific genes to orthogroups (Drosophila dataset 2)
drosophila2_stage_og <- lapply(
  drosophila2_deg_stage2,
  function(df) genes_to_og(rownames(df), gene2og, "drosophila")
)

names(drosophila2_stage_og) <- paste0("Drosophila2_", gsub("_vs_all", "", names(drosophila2_stage_og)))


# convert stage-specific genes to orthogroups (Blattella)
blattella_stage_og <- lapply(
  blattella_deg_stage2,
  function(df) genes_to_og(rownames(df), gene2og, "blattella")
)

names(blattella_stage_og) <- paste0("Blattella_", gsub("_vs_all", "", names(blattella_stage_og)))


# combine orthogroup sets from all species
stage_gene_sets <- c(
  harmonia_stage_og,
  plutella_stage_og,
  drosophila_stage_og,
  drosophila2_stage_og,
  blattella_stage_og
)

# ensure all orthogroups exist in gene2og table
stage_gene_sets <- lapply(stage_gene_sets, function(x) intersect(x, gene2og$og))

# check number of orthogroups per stage
sapply(stage_gene_sets, length)
          Harmonia_Larva         Harmonia_Prepupa            Harmonia_Pupa 
                     176                      157                      128 
          Plutella_Larva         Plutella_Prepupa            Plutella_Pupa 
                     169                      169                      220 
        Drosophila_Larva       Drosophila_Prepupa          Drosophila_Pupa 
                     195                      173                      266 
Drosophila2_Adult Female   Drosophila2_Adult Male       Drosophila2_Embryo 
                       4                       27                       87 
       Drosophila2_Larva         Drosophila2_Pupa   Blattella_Adult Female 
                      24                       34                        7 
          Blattella_Eggs         Blattella_Embryo          Blattella_Nymph 
                       9                       81                      169

Jaccard similarity

#shared genes / total unique genes across both stages
#-> mitigates bias from comparing groups with many DEGs vs comparing groups with less DEGs
#they may have similar overlap but it's different ratio
#because it's not all genes (orthogroups) but only DEGs orthogroups

# function calculating Jaccard similarity between two sets
jaccard <- function(a, b) {
  length(intersect(a, b)) / length(union(a, b))
}

sets <- names(stage_gene_sets)

# initialize similarity matrix
mat <- matrix(0, length(sets), length(sets))
rownames(mat) <- sets
colnames(mat) <- sets


# compute pairwise Jaccard similarity between stages
for(i in seq_along(sets)){
  for(j in seq_along(sets)){
    mat[i,j] <- jaccard(
      stage_gene_sets[[sets[i]]],
      stage_gene_sets[[sets[j]]]
    )
  }
}

# remove self-comparisons
diag(mat) <- NA

# define color scale for heatmap
breaks <- seq(0, 0.4, length.out = 100)

colors <- colorRampPalette(
  c("white", "#FDB863", "#E66101", "#B2182B")
)(99)


# plot heatmap of stage overlap
pheatmap(
  mat,
  clustering_distance_rows = "euclidean",
  clustering_distance_cols = "euclidean",
  color = colors,
  breaks = breaks,
  angle_col = 45,
  border_color = NA,
  na_col = "grey90",
  main = "Jaccard similarity of stage-specific DEGs"
)

# save heatmap to file
png("/home/cluster_user/Magisterka/plots/DEG_overlap_heatmap.png",
    width = 2200,
    height = 1800,
    res = 300)

pheatmap(
  mat,
  clustering_distance_rows = "euclidean",
  clustering_distance_cols = "euclidean",
  color = colors,
  breaks = breaks,
  angle_col = 45,
  border_color = NA,
  na_col = "grey90",
  main = "Jaccard similarity of stage-specific DEGs",
  fontsize_row = 7,
  fontsize_col = 7
)

dev.off()
png 
  3
